goal: Strengthen the guided router by applying limitations-doc mitigations §1, §2, §3, §4 and §7 as in-repo gateway config + harness scripts + docs, and add mutually-exclusive, profile-activatable gateway variants (litellm default, plus bifrost and helicone) each with dedicated deployment docs.

phases.goal: Route on difficulty, not length: enable the shipped neural router and make agentic/multi-turn tool-calling a hard escalation signal independent of prompt length — additively, without breaking ruflo-tiers.json's v1 schema.
definition_of_done: cmd: jq -e . ruflo-tiers.json >/dev/null  — existing alts file still valid JSON
cmd: jq -e '._meta.schema_version == 1' ruflo-tiers.json  — v1 schema preserved (no upstream-only v2 break)
grep: CLAUDE_FLOW_ROUTER_NEURAL in .env.example
grep: tier_floor|tier-floor|escalation in the new router-policy example file
cmd: jq -e . router-policy.example.json >/dev/null  — new policy file is valid JSON
grep: Tool-calling escalation in docs/guide/reference/tiers-and-routing.md
cmd: docker compose config -q  — compose still assembles

phases.goal: Lay the OpenTelemetry GenAI observability substrate the other mitigations depend on: gateway spans carrying gen_ai.* attributes, a collector in the compose stack, and the remaining-budget Prometheus gauges enabled.
definition_of_done: grep: otel in litellm-config.yaml  (callbacks list includes the OTel callback)
grep: prometheus_initialize_budget_metrics in litellm-config.yaml
grep: otel-collector in docker-compose.yml
cmd: docker compose config -q  — collector service is valid
cmd: yamllint -d relaxed otel-collector-config.yaml  — new collector config lints clean
grep: gen_ai.usage in docs/guide/reference/observability.md

phases.goal: Make budget steer, not just alert: express LiteLLM provider-budget routing so over-budget deployments are skipped (429) before hard-stop, track a token budget alongside USD, and plumb the cost-tracker's JSON into the routing decision (documented for the ruflo route() side).
definition_of_done: cmd: docker compose config -q  — budget config still renders
cmd: bash -n scripts/budget-snapshot.sh  — helper parses (no syntax errors)
cmd: shellcheck scripts/budget-snapshot.sh  — helper is clean
grep: Budget-steered routing in docs/guide/reference/budgets-and-tradeoffs.md
grep: 429 in docs/guide/reference/budgets-and-tradeoffs.md  — fail-closed backstop documented
grep:absent: TODO|FIXME in scripts/budget-snapshot.sh

phases.goal: Add in-band quality detection the error-based ladder can't provide: a FrugalGPT-style verify-then-escalate scorer on designated task classes (swap-averaged / rubric-anchored judge treated as noisy) plus a quality-regression harness that gates model swaps.
definition_of_done: cmd: bash -n scripts/verify-escalate.sh && bash -n scripts/quality-regression.sh
cmd: shellcheck scripts/verify-escalate.sh scripts/quality-regression.sh
cmd: scripts/quality-regression.sh --help  — prints usage and exits 0
cmd: jq -e '. ' tests/quality-prompts.jsonl >/dev/null 2>&1 || while read -r l; do echo "$l" | jq -e . >/dev/null; done < tests/quality-prompts.jsonl  — every corpus line is valid JSON
grep: swap-averaged|position-swap|rubric in scripts/verify-escalate.sh  — judge-bias mitigation present
grep:absent: TODO|FIXME in scripts/verify-escalate.sh scripts/quality-regression.sh

phases.goal: Make the gateway pluggable and mutually exclusive — exactly ONE of litellm (default) / bifrost / helicone runs at a time on the :4000 seam — and land the first alternative, Bifrost (Rust/Go, native OTel), mirroring the same tiers/budgets with a benchmark and dedicated docs. No default cutover: plain `docker compose up -d` still runs LiteLLM.
definition_of_done: cmd: docker compose config --services | grep -qx litellm  — litellm is the DEFAULT-active gateway (via .env COMPOSE_PROFILES=litellm)
cmd: ! docker compose config --services | grep -qx bifrost  — bifrost is NOT active by default
cmd: COMPOSE_PROFILES=bifrost docker compose config --services | grep -qx bifrost  — selecting bifrost activates it
cmd: ! (COMPOSE_PROFILES=bifrost docker compose config --services | grep -qx litellm)  — selecting bifrost DEACTIVATES litellm (mutual exclusion)
cmd: docker compose config -q && COMPOSE_PROFILES=bifrost docker compose config -q  — both resolutions assemble
grep: COMPOSE_PROFILES in .env.example  — the gateway selector is documented with litellm as the default
cmd: test -f docs/guide/reference/gateway-variants.md && grep -qi bifrost docs/guide/reference/gateway-variants.md
grep: tier-private in docs/guide/reference/gateway-variants.md  — privacy-pin behavior under the alternate gateway is documented
cmd: bash -n scripts/bench-gateway.sh && shellcheck scripts/bench-gateway.sh

phases.goal: Add Helicone AI Gateway (Rust, Apache-2.0; native OTel + rich budgets/caching) as the third mutually-exclusive gateway option under the Phase-5 selector, mirroring the tiers/budgets/privacy-pin, benchmarked, with its own deployment docs.
definition_of_done: cmd: COMPOSE_PROFILES=helicone docker compose config --services | grep -qx helicone  — helicone activates
cmd: ! (COMPOSE_PROFILES=helicone docker compose config --services | grep -qx litellm)  — and litellm is off (mutual exclusion holds)
cmd: ! (COMPOSE_PROFILES=helicone docker compose config --services | grep -qx bifrost)  — bifrost is off too (exactly one gateway)
cmd: COMPOSE_PROFILES=helicone docker compose config -q  — the Helicone variant assembles
cmd: docker compose config --services | grep -qx litellm  — default is STILL litellm (unchanged by adding helicone)
grep: helicone in docs/guide/reference/gateway-variants.md  — Helicone section present
grep:absent: TODO|FIXME in the Helicone config file
grep: tier-private in docs/guide/reference/gateway-variants.md  — privacy pin under Helicone documented (failover disabled)

phases.goal: Make the docs whole and honest again: flip the limitations 'honest list' + priority-order table to reflect what now ships, ensure every mitigation section, tiers/observability/budgets doc, and the three gateway variants cross-link cleanly, with no stale 'not solved' claims for §1–§4/§7.
definition_of_done: grep: APPLIED|shipped|available in docs/guide/reference/limitations-and-mitigations.md  — status annotations present for the applied mitigations
grep: gateway-variants in docs/guide/reference/limitations-and-mitigations.md  — item #7 points at the variants guide
grep: helicone in docs/guide/reference/gateway-variants.md  — three-way comparison present
cmd: yamllint -d relaxed docker-compose.yml litellm-config.yaml prometheus.yml grafana-datasources.yml  — all configs still lint clean
cmd: docker compose config -q && COMPOSE_PROFILES=bifrost docker compose config -q && COMPOSE_PROFILES=helicone docker compose config -q  — all three gateway resolutions assemble
cmd: docker compose config --services | grep -qx litellm  — litellm remains the default gateway

summary: §1/§2 overlay: router-policy.example.json + tiers-and-routing §1/§2 + limitations badges; ruflo-tiers v1 kept; 7/7 DoD green; review overclaims fixed to reference-policy wording.
